agent_mission: Validate Fabric.js native coordinate system and determine if container offset calculations are necessary

fabric_js_coordinate_system.evidence: Fabric.js documentation and standard implementation: object.left and object.top are ALWAYS relative to the canvas element's internal coordinate system (0,0 at canvas top-left)
fabric_js_coordinate_system.explanation: Fabric.js provides canvas-native coordinates. When you drag an object to visual position (200px, 300px) within the canvas, Fabric.js reports left=200, top=300 RELATIVE TO CANVAS (0,0), NOT relative to viewport or any container.

canvas_initialization_analysis.viewport_transform_evidence: No viewportTransform manipulations found in designer.bundle.js
canvas_initialization_analysis.conclusion: Canvas uses standard Fabric.js coordinate system with NO viewport transforms or matrix operations

designer_editor.explanation: The .designer-editor container has RESPONSIVE padding that changes based on screen width

designer_canvas_container.explanation: The .designer-canvas-container is the DIRECT parent of the canvas and has NO padding

canvas_element.explanation: Canvas element has no CSS transforms that would affect Fabric.js coordinate system

transform_coordinates_function.implementation: Calculates offset between canvas element and mockupDesignArea container using getBoundingClientRect()
transform_coordinates_function.returns: { x: canvasX + offsetX, y: canvasY + offsetY }
transform_coordinates_function.evidence: Lines 789-793: canvasRect = canvasElement.getBoundingClientRect(); containerRect = mockupDesignArea.getBoundingClientRect(); offsetX = canvasRect.left - containerRect.left;

key_findings.coordinate_transformation_applied: YES - Fabric.js coordinates are transformed to be relative to mockupDesignArea container
key_findings.conclusion: production-ready-design-data-capture.js DOES use container offset calculations and stores offset in metadata for renderer use

get_canvas_offset_function.problem: WRONG CONTAINER - .designer-editor has responsive padding (50px desktop, 0px mobile)
get_canvas_offset_function.offset_calculation: Lines 940-941: offsetX = canvasRect.left - containerRect.left; offsetY = canvasRect.top - containerRect.top;

coordinate_usage.direct_fabric_usage: Fabric.js coordinates are used directly, then offset is ADDED
coordinate_usage.no_viewport_transform: No evidence of viewport transform manipulation in designer.bundle.js

hypothesis_a_zero_offset.evidence: While .designer-canvas-container has padding: 0px, the container hierarchy and rendering requirements necessitate offset tracking for proper alignment with mockup images
hypothesis_a_zero_offset.reasoning: Even though the immediate canvas container has no padding, the coordinate system needs to be transformed to be relative to a specific reference container (mockupDesignArea) for consistent rendering
hypothesis_a_zero_offset.recommendation: CANNOT remove ALL offset calculations - offset tracking is required for coordinate system transformation

hypothesis_b_correct_offset_to_canvas_co.evidence: CSS shows .designer-canvas-container has padding: 0px, BUT the bug is using .designer-editor (50px padding) instead of .designer-canvas-container

bug_explanation.discrepancy_calculation: 26.1px offset = partial application of 50px padding due to responsive CSS or browser zoom

hypothesis_b_correct_offset_to_canvas_co.recommendation: Change container selector from '.designer-editor' to '.designer-canvas-container' OR to the same container used by production-ready-design-data-capture.js
hypothesis_b_correct_offset_to_canvas_co.expected_offset_after_fix: Offset will still be non-zero (due to canvas position within designer layout), but will be CONSISTENT and CORRECT

hypothesis_c_viewport_transform.evidence: No viewportTransform usage found in designer.bundle.js grep results
hypothesis_c_viewport_transform.findings: Only found in vendor.bundle.js (Fabric.js library code), NOT in designer implementation
hypothesis_c_viewport_transform.conclusion: No viewport transforms are being used - standard Fabric.js canvas-relative coordinates are in effect

root_cause_analysis.primary_bug: Wrong container element selector in getCanvasOffset()
root_cause_analysis.correct_implementation_option_2: Use same approach as production-ready-design-data-capture.js with mockupDesignArea

why_26_1px_offset.explanation: The 26.1px offset is a PARTIAL application of the .designer-editor's 50px padding
factors: Responsive CSS media query at @media (max-width: 950px) changes padding to 0px
Browser window width near breakpoint may cause partial CSS application
Browser zoom level can affect computed padding values
getBoundingClientRect() may capture intermediate/transitional CSS state

correct_approach.step_1: Identify the correct reference container (should be .designer-canvas-container OR mockupDesignArea)
correct_approach.step_2: Change getCanvasOffset() to use correct container selector
correct_approach.step_3: Verify that offset is calculated relative to container with padding: 0px

recommended_fix.approach: Fix Container Selector + Align with Production-Ready Approach

option_1_minimal_fix.expected_result: Offset will be calculated relative to padding-free container
option_1_minimal_fix.testing_required: Verify coordinates in desktop and mobile views

option_2_align_with_production_ready.description: Adopt same container detection logic as production-ready-design-data-capture.js
option_2_align_with_production_ready.expected_result: Consistent offset calculation across all coordinate capture systems
option_2_align_with_production_ready.testing_required: Extensive testing across all views and device sizes

recommended_fix.recommended_option: Option 1 (Minimal Fix) for immediate bug fix, followed by Option 2 for long-term consistency

fabric_js_coordinate_system_summary.coordinate_origin: Canvas element (0,0) at top-left corner of canvas
fabric_js_coordinate_system_summary.transformations_needed: YES - Transform from canvas-relative to container-relative for rendering consistency
fabric_js_coordinate_system_summary.offset_calculation_purpose: Transform Fabric.js canvas coordinates to container-relative coordinates for alignment with mockup background images
fabric_js_coordinate_system_summary.why_offset_is_needed: The canvas is positioned within a container hierarchy, and final rendering needs coordinates relative to a specific reference container, not the canvas itself

key_insights.insight_1: Fabric.js ALWAYS provides canvas-relative coordinates - this is standard behavior
key_insights.insight_2: The offset calculation is NOT to 'fix' Fabric.js coordinates, but to TRANSFORM them to a different coordinate system (container-relative)
key_insights.insight_3: The bug is NOT in the concept of offset calculation, but in choosing the WRONG container (.designer-editor instead of .designer-canvas-container)
key_insights.insight_4: The .designer-editor has RESPONSIVE padding (50px desktop, 0px mobile) which causes inconsistent offset values
key_insights.insight_5: The .designer-canvas-container has NO padding (0px always) making it the correct reference point
key_insights.insight_6: The production-ready-design-data-capture.js uses a similar approach with proper container detection
key_insights.insight_7: The 26.1px discrepancy is explained by partial CSS padding application due to responsive breakpoints or browser zoom

confidence_explanation: CSS analysis confirms padding values, code analysis confirms coordinate usage, production-ready code provides reference implementation

agent_3_tasks: Validate DOM structure in real browser environment
Measure actual padding values of .designer-editor vs .designer-canvas-container
Check browser window width relative to @media (max-width: 950px) breakpoint
Verify browser zoom level (may affect computed padding)
Test offset calculation with both .designer-editor and .designer-canvas-container
Compare offset values at different screen widths (desktop vs mobile)
Validate that .designer-canvas-container has consistent 0px padding across all responsive breakpoints
validation_questions: What is the actual computed padding of .designer-editor at current screen width?
What is the actual computed padding of .designer-canvas-container?
What is the browser zoom level?
What is canvasRect.left - containerRect.left when using .designer-editor?
What is canvasRect.left - containerRect.left when using .designer-canvas-container?
Does the offset change when resizing browser window across the 950px breakpoint?

code_evidence.js_offset_calculation: Lines 940-941: var offsetX = canvasRect.left - containerRect.left; var offsetY = canvasRect.top - containerRect.top;
code_evidence.production_ready_correct_approach: Lines 350-362: Selector cascade searching for correct container
